meaning: n.更新,复兴,恢复,续借,重申,补充
examples: They will discuss the possible renewal of diplomatic relations.
There has been very little renewal of personnel in higher education.
His avowed intention was to bring about reform and spiritual renewal for the church.
The new baseball season starts on April 1 and is, as always, a sign of hope and renewal.
So utilities have changed their focus from investments in growth to investments in renewal and replacement.
